title: helianthus atrorubens subsp. heterophyllus
intro: Helianthus atrorubens subsp. heterophyllus, also known as the purple coneflower or black-eyed Susan, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Helianthus atrorubens subsp. heterophyllus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
